questions.text: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error: The manager told the boys that one ought to work hard to earn his living.
options: A. The manager told the boys
C. work hard to earn his living

questions.text: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error: Scarcely had I arrived at the station than the train left the platform.
options: C. the train left the platform

questions.text:
Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If no substitution is required, select 'No improvement'. 
She is the _best and beautiful_ girl in our village.

Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If no substitution is required, select 'No improvement'. 
The patients _are waiting_ for the doctor since morning.
Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word: DEFER
Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word: PERILOUS
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word: MITIGATE
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word: OBSCURE
Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom: To bite the dust
options: B. To fail or be defeated
D. To be in a state of confusion

questions.text: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom: A storm in a teacup
options: A. A terrible tragedy that affects many
B. Enjoying warm tea in bad weather
C. A lot of anger or worry about something unimportant
D. A sudden and unexpected change in plan

questions.text: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given: A person who complies with accepted rules and customs
Select the word which means the same as the group of words given: The study of the origin and history of words
Select the correctly spelt word.
Select the incorrectly spelt word.
Select the most appropriate word to fill in the blank: The speaker did not properly space out his speech, but went on ______ one point only.
Select the most appropriate word to fill in the blank: The higher you climb, the ______ it gets.
Select the correct passive form of the given sentence: The mechanic repaired my car.
options: A. My car was being repaired by the mechanic.
B. My car was repaired by the mechanic.
C. My car is repaired by the mechanic.
D. My car has been repaired by the mechanic.

questions.text: Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence: He said, "I have passed the examination."
options: A. He said that he has passed the examination.
B. He said that he had passed the examination.
C. He said that I had passed the examination.
D. He said that he passed the examination.

questions.text:
Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the option that gives their correct order. 
A. It is also a source of inspiration. 
B. Literature is a mirror of society. 
C. Therefore, reading is essential for personal growth. 
D. It reflects the culture and values of a particular time.

Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the option that gives their correct order. 
A. He used his wealth to help the poor and needy. 
B. Once upon a time, there lived a wealthy merchant. 
C. Everyone in the village respected him for his generosity. 
D. He was well-known for his kindness and charity.

In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. 

Climate change is one of the most pressing (1) ______ of our time. It is primarily caused by the (2) ______ of greenhouse gases into the atmosphere. This has led to a (3) ______ in global temperatures. To mitigate these effects, countries must (4) ______ to reduce their carbon footprint. Renewable energy sources like solar and wind power offer a (5) ______ solution to this crisis.

Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number (1).
Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number (2).
Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number (3).
Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number (4).
Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number (5).
